title: Search an element in an array

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN and an element XXX. Your task is to find whether the array AAA contains the element XXX or not.


Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the size of array and the element to be searched.
The second line contains all the elements of array AAA


Output Format
Output "YES" if the element XXX is present in AAA, otherwise output "NO".
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 100000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two space‑separated integers N and X — the size of the array and the element to search for. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A₁, A₂, ..., A_N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if X appears in the array, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: The element 3 is present in the array, so the answer is YES.
The element 6 does not appear in the array, therefore the answer is NO.
The single element equals X, so the output is YES.

solution.approach: For each test case we read N and X, then scan the N array elements one by one. If any element equals X we set a flag. After reading the whole array we output "YES" if the flag is set, otherwise "NO". This linear scan is optimal because we must at least look at each element once in the worst case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(ΣN) where ΣN is the total number of array elements over all test cases (≤ 100000).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input stream.
